parentQuestion: In a spinal cord smear (multipolar neuron smear), which cell type has a large cell body with multiple processes extending outward?

scaffolds.q: What are the two main cell types in nervous tissue?
scaffolds.explain: Nervous tissue has two cell populations: NEURONS (signal transmitters) and NEUROGLIA (support cells). Neurons are the stars of the nervous system; glia are the supporting cast that outnumbers them 10:1.

wrongExplains.0: Red and white cells are blood cells. Nervous tissue contains neurons and glia.
wrongExplains.2: Epithelial and connective tissues are different tissue types entirely. Nervous tissue has neurons and glia.
wrongExplains.3: Muscle cells are in muscular tissue. The nerve fibers you see ARE parts of neurons.

scaffolds.q: What does 'multipolar' mean when describing a neuron?
options: The neuron has many processes (multiple dendrites and one axon)
scaffolds.explain: MULTIPOLAR = MANY POLES. These neurons have multiple dendrites (receiving branches) plus one axon (sending branch). Most neurons in the brain and spinal cord are multipolar.

wrongExplains.0: One process would be 'unipolar' — though true unipolar neurons are rare. Multipolar means many.
wrongExplains.1: Two processes describes 'bipolar' neurons (found in retina and olfactory epithelium). Multipolar means more than two.
wrongExplains.3: A neuron without processes couldn't communicate. All neurons have at least one process.

scaffolds.q: What is the large central portion of a neuron called?
scaffolds.explain: The CELL BODY (SOMA) is the neuron's 'headquarters' — containing the nucleus and most organelles. It's the large central portion you see in a neuron smear.

wrongExplains.0: The axon is a long process extending FROM the cell body — it's not the central portion.
wrongExplains.1: Dendrites are branching processes that receive signals — they extend from the cell body.
wrongExplains.3: The myelin sheath wraps around axons — it's not the central cell body.

scaffolds.q: What are the branching processes that receive signals and bring them toward the cell body?
scaffolds.explain: DENDRITES are the 'antenna' branches that RECEIVE signals and conduct them TOWARD the cell body. In multipolar neurons, there are typically many dendrites.

wrongExplains.0: Axons conduct signals AWAY from the cell body. Dendrites bring signals IN.
wrongExplains.2: Myelin sheaths are insulation around axons — they don't receive signals.
wrongExplains.3: Synaptic terminals are at the END of axons where signals are transmitted to the next cell.

scaffolds.q: In a spinal cord smear, larger cells with multiple radiating processes are most likely:
scaffolds.explain: The LARGER cells with MULTIPLE RADIATING PROCESSES are MULTIPOLAR NEURONS. The smaller cells scattered around them are neuroglia. Size and shape are your key identifiers.

wrongExplains.0: Neuroglia are smaller supporting cells — the large cells with radiating processes are neurons.
wrongExplains.2: Blood vessels would appear as tube-like structures with lumens — not star-shaped cells.
wrongExplains.3: Connective tissue fibers would appear as strands, not star-shaped cells with processes.
